title: Large language models
text: Large language models are a class of artificial intelligence systems that are trained on massive text corpora and can generate human-like text.

title: Retrieval augmented generation
text: Retrieval-augmented generation is a hybrid approach where a language model uses external documents retrieved from a knowledge base to improve accuracy.

title: Hallucination in AI
text: Model hallucination occurs when a model produces factually incorrect or unsupported statements that are not grounded in the input data.

title: Semantic similarity
text: Semantic similarity measures the proximity of meaning between two pieces of text using embeddings or vector-based comparisons.

title: BM25 ranking
text: BM25 is a ranking function used by search engines to rank matching documents according to query relevance.

title: Apple Inc
text: Apple Inc. is a technology company known for the iPhone, iPad, and Mac computers.

title: Apple fruit
text: An apple is a sweet fruit produced by the apple tree and is commonly eaten fresh or used in cooking.
